# Agent Pulse

> macOS menu bar app that monitors AI coding agent sessions and notifies you when any session needs attention.

Agent Pulse sits in your menu bar, watches all running Claude Code sessions, and fires a macOS notification the moment any session needs your attention. One click jumps to the exact terminal window.

## What it does

- Monitors all Claude Code sessions in real time
- Sends macOS notifications when a session requires input or permission
- Clicking a notification focuses the exact terminal tab (Terminal.app, iTerm2, VS Code, Warp, Ghostty)
- Shows all active sessions in a single menu bar popup with status, terminal name, and elapsed time
- Auto-installs Claude hooks — no manual configuration required
- 100% local, no telemetry, no network calls beyond localhost

## Status states

- **Idle** (grey) — session finished responding, ready for next input
- **Running** (blue) — session is actively executing tools or writing code
- **Input Required** (red) — session blocked, needs your approval or response

## Install

```
brew tap useagentpulse/agentpulse
brew install --cask agentpulse
```

Or download the DMG from: https://github.com/useagentpulse/agentpulse-app/releases/latest

## Requirements

- macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later
- Claude Code CLI (`claude` command)

## Supported terminals

Terminal.app, iTerm2, VS Code, Warp, Ghostty, IntelliJ IDEA

## Architecture

Built with Swift 6, SwiftUI, Hexagonal Architecture (Ports & Adapters). Extensible to support other AI agent CLIs (Gemini, Codex, Aider) by implementing a single protocol.

## Links

- Homepage: https://useagentpulse.github.io
